api/spec/packages/aip/src/llmcost.purpose: TypeSpec definitions for the LLM cost pricing domain: global synced price models, per-namespace override CRUD, and filter/pagination operations compiled to v3 OpenAPI. Two separate interfaces split global read-only prices from namespace-scoped override management.

patterns.name: Split interfaces by resource boundary
patterns.description: Separate LLMCostPricesOperations (global read-only prices) from LLMCostOverridesOperations (namespace-scoped CRUD) into distinct interfaces so Go generates separate handler groups.
patterns.example: interface LLMCostPricesOperations { list_prices(...); get_price(...); } interface LLMCostOverridesOperations { list_overrides(...); create_override(...); delete_override(...); }

patterns.name: deepObject filter params with explode
patterns.description: All filter parameters use @query(#{ style: "deepObject", explode: true }) so filter[field][op]=value query strings deserialize correctly.

patterns.name: Read-only visibility for all Price response fields
patterns.description: All Price model fields use @visibility(Lifecycle.Read) since prices are system-managed. OverrideCreate is a separate flat input model for writes — never mix read and write fields on the same model.

patterns.name: Enum for controlled string values
patterns.description: Use enum (not union) for closed string sets with known members like PriceSource. Use union only when open-ended or when needing @summary per variant.

patterns.name: Suppress repeated-prefix linter with justification
patterns.description: Fields like model_id/model_name, cache_read_per_token/cache_write_per_token, and effective_from/effective_to trigger the repeated-prefix-grouping linter; suppress with a specific reason string.
patterns.example: #suppress "@openmeter/api-spec-aip/repeated-prefix-grouping" "model_id and model_name should not be grouped"

key_file_guides.role: Declares LLMCostPricesOperations and LLMCostOverridesOperations interfaces with all HTTP endpoints and the ListPricesParamsFilter filter model.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Must import @typespec/http and use `using TypeSpec.Http;`. Sort is documented on list_prices but not list_overrides — keep consistent when extending. All operations require UnstableExtension and InternalExtension markers.

key_file_guides.role: Defines Price, ModelPricing, Provider, Model, OverrideCreate models and PriceSource enum.
key_file_guides.watch_for: OverrideCreate uses model_id (plain string) not a ULID — override creation is upsert (unique per provider+model+currency), not idempotent by ID. effective_from is required on OverrideCreate.

key_file_guides.role: Barrel: imports prices.tsp then operations.tsp in that order (models before operations).
key_file_guides.watch_for: Order matters — operations.tsp imports types from prices.tsp; the index must maintain this import order.

anti_patterns: Merging global price operations and override operations into a single interface
Using @visibility(Lifecycle.Create/Update) on Price model fields — prices are read-only responses; OverrideCreate is the separate write input model
Omitting effective_from on OverrideCreate — required to establish when the override takes effect
Using a ULID for OverrideCreate identification — overrides are upserted by provider+model+currency composite key, not by ID

decisions.decision: OverrideCreate is a flat input model separate from the Price response model
decisions.rationale: The write input (provider string, model_id, pricing) is structurally different from the full Price response which includes system-managed fields (id, source, created_at). Sharing one model would require complex visibility gymnastics.

decisions.decision: Two interfaces (Prices vs Overrides) rather than one combined interface
decisions.rationale: Global prices are read-only and system-synced; per-namespace overrides are user-managed CRUD. Separation makes handler responsibility boundaries clear and allows independent authorization policies.

code_examples.scenario: Add a new filter field to ListPricesParamsFilter

api/spec/packages/aip/src/meters.purpose: TypeSpec definitions for the Meters v3 API domain: Meter model (aggregation config, event_type, JSONPath dimensions), query request/result types, and CRUD+query operations. Primary constraint: query types live in query.tsp (not meter.tsp) for cross-namespace reuse.

patterns.name: union over enum for open enumerations
patterns.description: MeterAggregation and MeterQueryGranularity use `union` with string literals — not `enum` — so generated SDKs treat them as open string unions, preserving forward-compatibility when new aggregation types are added.
patterns.example: union MeterAggregation { sum: "sum", count: "count", unique_count: "unique_count", avg: "avg", min: "min", max: "max", latest: "latest" }

patterns.name: @sharedRoute for content-negotiated variants
patterns.description: The meter query endpoint has two operations (JSON and CSV) sharing the same POST URL via @sharedRoute. Content type is discriminated by @header contentType. The CSV variant (queryCsv) intentionally omits @body to avoid anyOf in generated OpenAPI.
patterns.example: @post @operationId("query-meter") @sharedRoute query(@path meterId: Shared.ULID, @body request: MeterQueryRequest): { @header contentType: "application/json"; @body _: MeterQueryResult; } | ...

patterns.name: @example decorator on complex models
patterns.description: Models with complex structure (Meter, MeterQueryRequest, MeterQueryRow, MeterQueryResult) include @example(#{ ... }) decorators to provide concrete OpenAPI examples.
patterns.example: @example(#{ id: "01G65Z755AFWAKHE12NY0CQ9FH", key: "tokens_total", aggregation: "sum", event_type: "prompt", value_property: "$.tokens" }) model Meter { ... }

patterns.name: query.tsp isolation for reusable query types
patterns.description: MeterQueryRequest, MeterQueryResult, MeterQueryRow, MeterQueryFilters, and MeterQueryGranularity are isolated in query.tsp so other namespaces (e.g. features/cost.tsp) can import them.
patterns.example: // features/cost.tsp imports query.tsp to reuse MeterQueryRequest: import "../meters/query.tsp";

patterns.name: @visibility for immutable vs. updatable fields
patterns.description: Meter fields aggregation, event_type, value_property carry only Lifecycle.Read + Lifecycle.Create (immutable after creation). The dimensions field uniquely adds Lifecycle.Update — it is the only mutable Meter field.
patterns.example: @visibility(Lifecycle.Read, Lifecycle.Create, Lifecycle.Update) dimensions?: Record<string>; // vs. @visibility(Lifecycle.Read, Lifecycle.Create) aggregation: MeterAggregation;

key_file_guides.role: Declares MetersOperations (CRUD) and MetersQueryOperations (query + queryCsv). Both query methods share operationId 'query-meter' via @sharedRoute.
key_file_guides.watch_for: queryCsv intentionally omits @body on the request. Do not add a request body back — it produces anyOf in the generated OpenAPI spec.

key_file_guides.role: Core Meter model with aggregation type, event_type, optional value_property, and dimensions map.
key_file_guides.watch_for: aggregation and event_type are Create+Read only (immutable after creation). value_property is optional only for count aggregation.

key_file_guides.role: All meter query I/O types. MeterQueryFilters.dimensions uses Record<Shared.QueryFilterStringMapItem>; MeterQueryRow.dimensions is Record<string> (output only).
key_file_guides.watch_for: group_by_dimensions has @maxItems(100). Do not confuse the input filter map (QueryFilterStringMapItem) with the output dimensions map (plain Record<string>).

key_file_guides.role: Barrel — imports meter.tsp then operations.tsp. query.tsp is NOT imported here; it is imported directly by operations.tsp and external namespaces needing only query types.
key_file_guides.watch_for: If adding a new .tsp file, import it in index.tsp or it is invisible to consumers using the barrel import.

anti_patterns: Using `enum` instead of `union` for MeterAggregation or MeterQueryGranularity — breaks forward-compatibility of generated SDKs
Adding a request body to the queryCsv operation — intentionally omitted to prevent anyOf in OpenAPI output
Putting query types in meter.tsp — they belong in query.tsp for cross-namespace reuse by features/cost.tsp
Setting dimensions field visibility to Read+Create only — dimensions is the one updatable Meter field and must keep Lifecycle.Update

decisions.decision: CSV export as @sharedRoute variant of the same POST endpoint rather than a separate GET
decisions.rationale: Content negotiation (Accept: text/csv) on a single URL is idiomatic REST; sharedRoute avoids duplicating route+operationId while allowing separate TypeSpec response type shapes for JSON vs. CSV.

decisions.decision: Query types isolated in query.tsp separate from meter.tsp
decisions.rationale: features/cost.tsp needs Meters.MeterQueryRequest without pulling in the full Meter model and CRUD operations; isolation enables targeted cross-namespace imports.

code_examples.scenario: Adding a new meter operation (e.g. bulk-delete)

api/spec/packages/aip/src/productcatalog.purpose: TypeSpec definitions for the ProductCatalog v3 API domain: Plan (versioned phases + rate cards), Addon, PlanAddon associations, Price polymorphism (free/flat/unit/graduated/volume), UnitConfig conversions, and CRUD+lifecycle operations. Primary constraint: Plan/Addon version and lifecycle status fields are server-managed Read-only — never set by clients.

patterns.name: Versioned resources with server-computed status lifecycle
patterns.description: Plan and Addon carry version (@visibility(Lifecycle.Read), @minValue(1)), effective_from/effective_to (Read-only), and status enum (draft/active/archived/scheduled). Status is never settable by clients — it is derived from effective dates on the server.

patterns.name: Lifecycle action sub-routes as @post operations
patterns.description: State transitions (publish, archive) are modeled as POST sub-routes (/{id}/publish, /{id}/archive) on the same interface, returning Shared.UpdateResponse<T>. Not nested interfaces or PATCH fields.

patterns.name: Discriminated union for Price polymorphism
patterns.description: Price and PriceUsageBased are @discriminated(#{ envelope: "none", discriminatorPropertyName: "type" }) unions. Every variant carries a `type` field matching PriceType. PriceUsageBased is a sub-union (unit/graduated/volume only) for usage-based pricing.
patterns.example: @discriminated(#{ envelope: "none", discriminatorPropertyName: "type" }) union Price { free: PriceFree, flat: PriceFlat, unit: PriceUnit, graduated: PriceGraduated, volume: PriceVolume }

patterns.name: validation_errors as Read-only list on all catalog resources
patterns.description: Plan, Addon, and PlanAddon all carry @visibility(Lifecycle.Read) validation_errors?: ProductCatalogValidationError[] to surface draft-state validation issues. Any new catalog resource must include this field.

patterns.name: UpsertRequest/UpsertResponse for PUT operations on versioned resources
patterns.description: PUT operations (update-plan, update-addon, update-plan-addon) use Shared.UpsertRequest<T> body and Shared.UpsertResponse<T> return type — not CreateRequest/UpdateResponse. Responses also include Common.Gone for archived resources.
patterns.example: @put updatePlan(@path planId: Shared.ULID, @body plan: Shared.UpsertRequest<Plan>): Shared.UpsertResponse<Plan> | Common.ErrorResponses | Common.Gone | Common.NotFound;

patterns.name: PickProperties spread for partial resource base models
patterns.description: PlanPhase and RateCard use PickProperties<Shared.ResourceWithKey, "key" | "name" | "description" | "labels"> instead of the full ResourceWithKey spread, excluding id and timestamps because phases/rate cards have no independent lifecycle.
patterns.example: model PlanPhase { ...PickProperties<Shared.ResourceWithKey, "key" | "name" | "description" | "labels">; duration?: Shared.ISO8601Duration; rate_cards: RateCard[]; }

key_file_guides.role: Price discriminated union and all variant models. PriceUsageBased is a sub-union (unit/graduated/volume) for usage-based-only price types on a rate card.
key_file_guides.watch_for: PriceTier requires at least one of flat_price or unit_price (documented, not TypeSpec-enforced). PriceFlat embedded inside PriceTier does not carry a payment_term.

key_file_guides.role: RateCard model: key, feature reference, billing_cadence (null = one-time), price union, payment_term, commitments, discounts, tax_config.
key_file_guides.watch_for: billing_cadence nullable means one-time charge (only valid with flat prices). SettlementMode enum is defined here but verify usage before referencing.

key_file_guides.role: UnitConfig model for raw-to-billing-unit conversion (divide/multiply + rounding) and InvoiceUsageQuantityDetail for invoice audit trail.
key_file_guides.watch_for: rounding defaults to None; precision is only meaningful when rounding != none. applied_unit_config on InvoiceUsageQuantityDetail is a Read-only snapshot for historical correctness.

key_file_guides.role: Plan and PlanPhase models, PlanStatus enum, ProductCatalogValidationError model reused across all catalog entities.
key_file_guides.watch_for: phases has @minItems(1) — at least one phase required. billing_cadence on Plan is Create+Read only (immutable after creation).

key_file_guides.role: PlanOperations, AddonOperations, PlanAddonOperations interfaces. PlanAddon routes nest under /{planId}/addons/ and require both planId and planAddonId path params.
key_file_guides.watch_for: All operations carry UnstableExtension + InternalExtension. PlanAddon upsert uses both path params.

key_file_guides.role: Addon model mirroring Plan structure: key, version, instance_type (single/multiple), currency, effective dates, rate_cards, validation_errors.
key_file_guides.watch_for: AddonInstanceType affects max_quantity validation on PlanAddon — single instance addons must omit max_quantity.

anti_patterns: Setting status, effective_from, or effective_to in Create/Update requests — they are server-computed Read-only fields
Using Shared.CreateRequest/UpdateResponse for PUT plan/addon operations — must use UpsertRequest/UpsertResponse
Omitting validation_errors from a new catalog resource model — required for draft-state UX
Using RateCard.price with PriceFree for usage-based rate cards — use the PriceUsageBased union (unit/graduated/volume only)
Adding a full ...Shared.Resource spread to PlanPhase or RateCard — these are sub-resources; use PickProperties for key/name/description/labels

decisions.decision: UnitConfig is a separate model on RateCard rather than embedded in price types
decisions.rationale: The same feature can be billed in different converted units across plans (bytes->GB, seconds->hours); keeping unit conversion at the rate card level decouples it from the pricing model and allows reuse.

decisions.decision: Plan/Addon versioning is server-managed (version field Read-only, incremented on update)
decisions.rationale: Prevents clients from accidentally creating version conflicts; the server controls the version chain ensuring subscription compatibility checks remain valid.

decisions.decision: Lifecycle actions (publish, archive) as POST sub-routes, not PATCH status fields
decisions.rationale: State transitions have server-side preconditions (e.g. publish requires no validation_errors); explicit action endpoints make transition semantics discoverable and allow rich error responses.

code_examples.scenario: Adding a new lifecycle action (e.g. suspend) to PlanOperations
